Research Notes — Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG): RAG systems combine a parametric language model with a non-parametric retrieval component to ground generation in retrieved documents, reducing hallucination and enabling the model to access up-to-date information without retraining. The retriever (typically a dense bi-encoder like DPR) retrieves top-k passages from a document store, which are concatenated with the query and fed to the generator (e.g., a seq2seq model). Lewis et al. (2020) demonstrated that RAG outperforms pure parametric models on knowledge-intensive NLP tasks while remaining competitive on extractive QA benchmarks. Key challenges include retrieval quality, context window limitations for long documents, and faithfulness of generation to retrieved passages.
